title: Passkeys were invented by engineers with zero understanding of consumer brain
titleZh: 万能钥匙是由对消费者大脑零了解的工程师发明的
author: ksec
relevanceReason: 命中 1 个 AI/科技关键词。
summaryRaw: Don't miss what's happeningPeople on X are the first to know.
summaryZh: Don't miss what's happeningPeople on X are the first to know.

comments.author: gortok
comments.textRawHtml:
I do not know how to use a Passkey in a way that won’t impede how I log in to systems. I’ve been in tech for 26 years, and I understand the Public/private key behind what a Passkey is. Here’s what I don’t understand:

I access a website through at least four different devices (my iPad, iPhone, Windows Desktop computer, and MacBook Pro) and three different browsers on each device (Brave, Firefox, Safari) , and I use LastPass. If I accidentally set up a passkey on my phone (let’s say I use Safari one day instead of my go-to, Brave), can I still log in without that passkey on other devices? Is there a way to ensure that passkey can be used on other devices? Can I add another passkey on another device? How many passkeys can I set up for a particular site/app? I have at least 6 different combination of browser/devices in use.

I don’t want to use Passkeys because I don’t the answers to those questions, and I don’t know whether each website/app that has set up Passkeys has decided the answers to those questions in the same way as the others. For now, I’m going to stick with LastPass and use Passwords; because no matter whether I lose my device or not or whether I’m on my own devices or not, I can be sure I’ll be able to get into a site/app.

Edit: One final consideration, my spouse and I share user/name passwords for some things (notably Pandora and our Amazon Prime account) since they don’t handle things like family logins well; how do both my wife and I use amazon or Pandora with passkeys? Do we each set up passkeys? How do I get her Pass if that’s not an option?
comments.textZhHtml:
我不知道如何以不妨碍我登录系统的方式使用密钥。我在科技领域工作了 26 年，我了解密钥背后的公钥/私钥。以下是我不明白的地方：

我通过至少四种不同的设备（我的 iPad、iPhone、Windows 台式电脑和 MacBook Pro）以及每台设备上的三种不同的浏览器（Brave、Firefox、Safari）访问网站，并且我使用 LastPass。 如果我不小心在手机上设置了密码（假设有一天我使用 Safari 而不是我常用的 Brave），我是否仍然可以在其他设备上没有该密码的情况下登录？有没有办法确保密钥可以在其他设备上使用？ 我可以在另一台设备上添加另一个密钥吗？我可以为特定网站/应用程序设置多少个密钥？我至少使用了 6 种不同的浏览器/设备组合。

我不想使用 Passkeys，因为我不知道这些问题的答案，而且我不知道每个设置了 Passkeys 的网站/应用程序是否都以与其他网站/应用程序相同的方式决定了这些问题的答案。 现在，我将坚持使用 LastPass 并使用密码；因为无论我是否丢失设备，或者是否使用自己的设备，我都可以确定我能够进入网站/应用程序。

编辑：最后一个考虑因素是，我和我的配偶共享某些内容的用户/名称密码（特别是 Pandora 和我们的 Amazon Prime 帐户），因为他们不能很好地处理家庭登录等问题；我和我的妻子如何通过密钥使用亚马逊或潘多拉？我们每个人都设置密钥吗？如果这不是一个选择，我如何获得她的通行证？

children.author: thecombjelly
children.textRawHtml:
A potentially good idea got corrupted by vendors, password managers, browsers, etc trying to assert control. I'm also an engineer and I find the UI around passkeys entirely unclear, but it doesn't have to be that way. It seems like everyone wants to be _the_ password manager for all your passkeys. They don't want to make it easy to understand that is what they are doing though, they just happily offer to "handle it for you".

My non-technical friends are extremely confused by passkeys and if they should use them and how to use them and I honestly don't have very good answers. It is a mess. I don't believe an inherit mess, but one created by the companies and projects trying to take advantage of the new system.
children.textZhHtml:
一个潜在的好主意被试图主张控制权的供应商、密码管理器、浏览器等破坏了。我也是一名工程师，我发现密钥周围的用户界面完全不清楚，但它不一定是这样的。似乎每个人都想成为所有万能钥匙的密码管理器。他们不想让人们容易理解他们正在做的事情，他们只是很高兴地提出“为你处理它”。

我的非技术朋友对万能钥匙非常困惑，他们是否应该使用它们以及如何使用它们，老实说我没有很好的答案。真是一团糟。我不相信继承的混乱，而是由试图利用新系统的公司和项目造成的混乱。

children.author: thewebguyd
children.textRawHtml:
> It seems like everyone wants to be _the_ password manager for all your passkeys.

Which defeats part of the point of passkeys in the first place in that they are supposed to be device-bound, the private key held in the TPM or secure enclave or whatever other security chip, mathematically non-exportable. Storing all your private keys in a cloud vault still leaves you exposed to potential credential theft if your vault gets compromised.

Every device is supposed to have its own unique private key, stored in TPM, released only when passing the user challenge (biometrics or pin, or a yubikey).
children.textZhHtml:
> 似乎每个人都想成为所有万能钥匙的密码管理器。

这首先就违背了万能钥匙的部分要点，因为它们应该是设备绑定的，私钥保存在 TPM 或安全飞地或任何其他安全芯片中，从数学上讲是不可导出的。如果您的保管库受到威胁，将所有私钥存储在云保管库中仍然会让您面临潜在的凭据盗窃风险。

每个设备都应该有自己唯一的私钥，存储在 TPM 中，仅在通过用户质询（生物识别或 PIN 码或 yubikey）时释放。

children.author: dotancohen
children.textRawHtml:
> Every device is supposed to have its own unique private key, stored in TPM, released only when passing the user challenge (biometrics or pin, or a yubikey).
I have just shy of 2000 site credentials in Keepass. Let's assume that they were all Passkeys.

1) When I buy a new device, how do I create 2000 new Passkeys for that device?

2) Can I still do that if I don't have access to the old device? Maybe it was destroyed, stolen, or lost.

3) How about if the new device is from a different vendor than the original device? E.g. switching from Apple to Android?
children.textZhHtml:
> 每个设备都应该有自己唯一的私钥，存储在 TPM 中，仅在通过用户挑战（生物识别或 PIN 或 yubikey）时释放。
我在 Keepass 中只有不到 2000 个站点凭据。假设它们都是密钥。

1) 当我购买新设备时，如何为该设备创建 2000 个新密钥？

2) 如果我无法访问旧设备，我还能这样做吗？也许它被毁坏、被盗或丢失。

3) 如果新设备来自与原始设备不同的供应商怎么办？例如。从苹果切换到安卓？

children.author: crote
children.textRawHtml:
The original U2F tokens didn't suffer from this problem that much. It contained one secret, it relied on the website to store and return an opaque website-and-account-specific blob, which was decrypted by the token and used to sign the login request. It would be reasonably easy to expand this in a way where the server also stores a backup credential for use by your second/third/whatever hardware token, encrypted with an asymmetric key during the enrollment of the primary token.

But this went out of the window when some genius decided that usernames were too complicated, so Passkeys had to be discoverable, which means they have to be fully stored token-side. Which of course has the nice side benefit of essentially killing hardware tokens and forcing people into using their Android/iOS/Windows device for it.
children.textZhHtml:
原始 U2F 代币并没有受到太多这个问题的困扰。它包含一个秘密，它依赖网站来存储和返回不透明的网站和帐户特定的 blob，该 blob 由令牌解密并用于签署登录请求。通过服务器还存储备份凭证以供第二个/第三个/任何硬件令牌使用的备份凭据，在主令牌注册期间使用非对称密钥进行加密，这将相当容易进行扩展。

但是，当一些天才认为用户名太复杂时，这种情况就被排除在外了，因此密码必须是可发现的，这意味着它们必须完全存储在令牌端。当然，这还有一个很好的副作用，那就是基本上消除了硬件令牌并迫使人们使用他们的 Android/iOS/Windows 设备。

children.author: omnifischer
children.textRawHtml: This. I would have loved if this original u2f existed. Google has even removed this from their accounts.
children.textZhHtml: 这个。如果这个原始的 u2f 存在的话我会很高兴。谷歌甚至已将其从他们的帐户中删除。

children.author: chimeracoder
children.textRawHtml:
> But this went out of the window when some genius decided that usernames were too complicated, so Passkeys had to be discoverable, which means they have to be fully stored token-side. Which of course has the nice side benefit of essentially killing hardware tokens and forcing people into using their Android/iOS/Windows device for it.

This part of your comment is incorrect. Discoverable Webauthn absolutely can be used with hardware tokens like Yubikeys.
children.textZhHtml:
> 但是，当一些天才认为用户名太复杂，因此密钥必须是可发现的，这意味着它们必须完全存储在令牌端时，这就被排除在外了。当然，这有一个很好的附带好处，即基本上消除了硬件令牌并迫使人们使用他们的 Android/iOS/Windows 设备。

您的这部分评论是不正确的。可发现的 Webauthn 绝对可以与 Yubikeys 等硬件令牌一起使用。

children.author: Itoldmyselfso
children.textRawHtml:
Passkey comprises of public key that the website you created it on holds, and the private key you store on your pw manager or in the TPM/secure element. As long as you can copy the private key to the new device, you don't need to recreate the whole passkey. In your case, you would just need access to a backup of the Keepass database in case you lose the device.

The biggest point of confusion in my opinion comes from Windows especially having lacked a way (and kind of still does) to save the private key of a passkey to your password manager, defaulting to saving it to Windows Hello, which saves the private key to your PC's TPM. In this scenario you can no longer easily copy the private key to other devices, and if you lose that Windows PC, you also lose the private key and the whole passkey as a result.
children.textZhHtml:
密码由您创建的网站持有的公钥以及您存储在密码管理器或 TPM/安全元件中的私钥组成。只要您可以将私钥复制到新设备，就无需重新创建整个密钥。就您而言，您只需要访问 Keepass 数据库的备份，以防丢失

在我看来，最大的困惑来自于 Windows，尤其是它缺乏一种方法（现在仍然如此）将密钥的私钥保存到密码管理器中，默认将其保存到 Windows Hello，后者将私钥保存到 PC 的 TPM 中。在这种情况下，您无法再轻松地将私钥复制到其他设备，并且如果您丢失了该 Windows PC，您也会因此丢失私钥和整个密钥。

children.author: pseudalopex
children.textRawHtml: People who say users should not be able to export keys are not confused. They believe users should not be able to export keys.
children.textZhHtml: 那些说用户不应该能够导出密钥的人并不感到困惑。他们认为用户不应该能够导出密钥。

children.author: Itoldmyselfso
children.textRawHtml: I was referring to confusion experienced by people new to the passkeys creating passkeys for the first time ("Where am I saving this to?", "How do I store this in my password manager?").
children.textZhHtml: 我指的是第一次创建密钥的新手所经历的困惑（“我将其保存到哪里？”，“如何将其存储在我的密码管理器中？”）。

children.author: ivlad
children.textRawHtml:
You don’t. You don’t store anything at all.

On registration, a keypair is generated, then the private key is encrypted with the long-term key burned into your security key fob or hardware. The encrypted blob is sent to the server and stored there.

On authentication, after you enter your login, the server sends the encrypted blob and your security key tries to decrypt it with the long-term key it has. If it succeeds, it then request a challenge from the servers, signs it along with the server name and timestamp and sends back to the server. Server validates the signature and if it’s good, log you in.

Expanded: As long you as the user has the security key fob, you can login. You should have 2.
children.textZhHtml:
你不知道。您根本不存储任何内容。

注册时，会生成密钥对，然后使用刻录到安全密钥卡或硬件中的长期密钥对私钥进行加密。加密的 blob 被发送到服务器并存储在那里。

在进行身份验证时，在您输入登录名后，服务器会发送加密的 blob，并且您的安全密钥会尝试使用其拥有的长期密钥对其进行解密。如果成功，它会向服务器请求质询，将其与服务器名称和时间戳一起签名，然后发送回服务器。服务器验证签名，如果签名正确，则登录。

扩展：只要您作为用户拥有安全密钥，就可以登录。您应该有 2 个。

children.author: doodlesdev
children.textRawHtml:
> You don’t. You don’t store anything at all.

Yes, you do.

Whenever a website offers to create a passkey, it could end up in any of these:

- Samsung's Password Manager (if using a Samsung phone)

- Apple's Keychain (if using an iPhone)

- Google Password Manager

- Your operating system's keychain

- A bespoke password manager (e.g., Bitwarden or LastPass)

- Your hardware key

Most users do not have a security key fob. Instead, the proposal being mostly pushed is the idea that users can store keys on their own smartphones, making use of the modern TPM and chip security. Most of the discussion here revolves around that idea: "What if I lose my phone? What if I switch phones?" and that's why the problems seem so obvious to you.

I absolutely agree the best solution is to use hardware keys, but I'll admit it's cumbersome if I need them for hundreds of accounts (which I do have), having to register both for every website and praying that I never lose both at the same time in the case there's no viable recovery flow for some of the accounts. Also, most of these hardware keys are limited to 25 or 100 resident keys, which again makes them unable to substitute passwords. Observe the usage of the term resident keys: passkeys do rely on the private key being stored on the hardware key, as that allows discovery.
children.textZhHtml:
> 你不知道。您根本不存储任何内容。

是的，您会这样做。

每当网站提供创建密钥时，它最终可能会出现在以下任何一个中：

- 三星的密码管理器（如果使用三星手机）

- Apple 的钥匙串（如果使用 iPhone）

- Google 密码管理器

- 您操作系统的钥匙串

- 定制的密码管理器（例如 Bitwarden 或 LastPass）

- 您的硬件密钥

大多数用户没有安全密钥卡。相反，主要推动的提案是用户可以利用现代 TPM 和芯片安全性将密钥存储在自己的智能手机上。这里的大部分讨论都围绕这个想法：“如果我丢了手机怎么办？如果我换手机怎么办？”这就是为什么问题对你来说如此明显。

我绝对同意最好的解决方案是使用硬件密钥，但我承认，如果我需要为数百个帐户（我确实有）使用它们，这会很麻烦，必须为每个网站注册这两个帐户，并祈祷我永远不会同时丢失这两个帐户，以防某些帐户没有可行的恢复流程。此外，大多数这些硬件密钥仅限于 25 或 100 个常驻密钥，这又使得它们无法替换密码。观察术语“驻留密钥”的用法：万能密钥确实依赖于存储在硬件密钥上的私钥，因为这允许发现。

children.author: ivlad
children.textRawHtml:
I was talking about the non-resident FIDO keys. “Passkey” term is meaningless unfortunately because FIDO Alliance did not define it initially, it was a marketing term invented by Apple and then re-introduced (or shoved down the throat) by the FIDO alliance.

In non-resident keys scenario you don’t store anything and from what I see there is no security downside of using non-resident keys.

Loosing both (or multiple) security keys is like loosing all your car or home keys. Very inconvenient, agreed.

Anyway, I think we can agree that FIDO authentication protocol implementation is a mess. Apple and Google made it messy because they wanted to lock down users to their platforms and then password managers followed. As a result, the current implementation is not more secure than “login with Apple” or “login with Google”.
children.textZhHtml:
我正在谈论非驻留 FIDO 密钥。不幸的是，“密钥”一词毫无意义，因为 FIDO 联盟最初并未对其进行定义，它是 Apple 发明的营销术语，然后由 FIDO 联盟重新引入（或强行强行推行）。

在非居民密钥场景中，您不会存储任何内容，从我看来，使用非居民密钥不会带来安全问题。

丢失两个（或多个）安全密钥就像丢失所有汽车或家庭钥匙一样。非常不方便，同意。

无论如何，我想我们可以同意FIDO认证协议的实现是一团糟。苹果和谷歌让事情变得混乱，因为他们想将用户锁定在他们的平台上，然后密码管理器也随之而来。因此，当前的实现并不比“使用 Apple 登录”或“使用 Google 登录”更安全。

children.author: pseudalopex
children.textRawHtml: dotancohen would not be allowed to store Passkeys private keys in a Keepass database in the case thewebguyd specified. Relying parties would block password managers which allowed this.
children.textZhHtml: 在 thewebguyd 指定的情况下，不允许 dotancohen 将 Passkeys 私钥存储在 Keepass 数据库中。依赖方会阻止允许这样做的密码管理器。

children.author: zdp7
children.textRawHtml:
1) Install a keepass client and use it for passkeys.

2) See #1

3) See #1

KeepassXC supports passkeys. They suggest a couple mobile apps that also do.
children.textZhHtml:
1) 安装keepass 客户端并将其用作密钥。

2) 请参阅#1

3) 请参阅#1

KeepassXC 支持密钥。他们推荐了一些也可以的移动应用程序。

children.author: ilchalpenl
children.textRawHtml:
> Let's assume that they were all Passkeys.

Lets assume I put all the effort to explain.

I will give you a better example.

- Lets say you have a google account with pixel phone

- You are using it and added 1000 passkeys

- All are synced to your Google account

- Destroy and Get a new phone. Login to your google account with recovery code.

- All your passkeys are in your phone again.

There are plans in fidoalliance.org to make it portable. Pretty sure you are still not going to move to it.
children.textZhHtml:
> 假设它们都是密钥。

假设我已尽力解释。

我会给您一个更好的示例。

- 假设您有一个带有 Pixel 手机的 Google 帐户

- 您正在使用它并添加了 1000 个密钥

- 所有密钥均同步到您的 Google 帐户

- 销毁并获取一部新手机。使用恢复代码登录您的 Google 帐户。

- 您的所有密钥都再次位于您的手机中。

fidoalliance.org 计划使其便于携带。 很确定您仍然不会转向它。

children.author: sandmn
children.textRawHtml: It works fine until Google suddenly decides that your new device is not you and asks you to confirm login from previous device...
children.textZhHtml: 一切正常，直到 Google 突然判定您的新设备不是您，并要求您确认从以前的设备登录...

children.author: account42
children.textRawHtml: Or until Google decides that you did a wrongthink and bans your account, including locking you out of your old phone.
children.textZhHtml: 或者直到 Google 判定您的想法错误并禁止您的帐户，包括将您锁定在旧手机之外。

children.author: marysol5
children.textRawHtml: Oh look, the strawman is a victim

children.author: ilchalpenl
children.textRawHtml:
See.. the sad thing for doomsayers like you is that if even 1% of users were affected then there would be mass exodus from Gmail. It does not.

Yes, you don't use Gmail so it does not matter. You may be don't know. And often even average Joe some how has a oldphone or iPad that has oldgmail account there or logged into wife's phone or phone based SMS whatever.

it is ok to hate passkeys or google or love only self-hosted but let others do what they want.

I have been user of solokeys (since the first one) - only opensource hardware keys. works for me...

But if you go to the local highstreet then there are tons of people doing this screenrepair etc just to recover the account. Average Joe doesnot mind paying for that. Even will give the repair guy full password to transfer all data from old to new phone.
children.textZhHtml:
瞧……对于像您这样的末日论者来说，可悲的是，即使只有 1% 的用户受到影响，Gmail 也会出现大规模用户流失。不会。

是的，您不使用 Gmail，所以没关系。你可能不知道。甚至普通的乔也常常拥有一台旧手机或 iPad，其中有 oldgmail 帐户或登录到妻子的手机或基于手机的短信等。

讨厌密钥或谷歌或只喜欢自托管但让其他人做他们想做的事情是可以的。

我一直是 solokeys 的用户（从第一个开始） - 只有开源硬件密钥。 对我有用...

但是如果你去当地的商业街，那么会有很多人进行屏幕修复等操作，只是为了恢复帐户。普通乔并不介意为此付出代价。甚至会给维修人员完整的密码，以将所有数据从旧手机转移到新手机。
